skill.name: ai-audio-generation
skill.description: AI audio generation for agents through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted creative runtime. Use when a prompt should become music, sound, or audio without provider credentials, OAuth, local runtime, or per-provider billing. Start with the no-spend guide, choose an audio model, and keep durable hosted audio URLs, recoverable jobs, cost receipts, stable JSON, payments, and feedback in one loop.
skill.summary: Generate AI audio, music, and sound effects from text prompts with zero-setup hosted runtime and durable media URLs.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The skill file contains only documentation with bash command examples for installing and using the image-skill CLI. All flagged external_commands are documented npx commands for user execution, not actual code execution in the skill. All network URLs point to the skill's own domain (image-skill.com) for documentation and API references. No malicious intent, credential harvesting, data exfiltration, or obfuscation detected. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives triggered by YAML documentation text, not actual cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Documentation includes CLI commands that require user execution
low_findings.description: The skill file contains bash command examples (npx, export, mkdir) that users would execute manually. These are standard CLI installation and usage instructions, not actual code execution within the skill. No security risk as commands are transparent and user-initiated.

content.user_title: Generate AI Audio from Text Prompts
content.value_statement: Creating AI-generated audio requires complex setup with multiple providers and API keys. This skill provides zero-setup access to audio generation through a single hosted runtime with built-in cost controls and durable media storage.
actual_capabilities: Generate music, sound effects, and audio from text prompts using fal.stable-audio-25-text-to-audio model
Run no-spend guide commands to explore capabilities before committing to paid generation
Create durable hosted audio URLs that persist and can be cited by future agents
Track recoverable jobs with cost receipts and stable JSON output
Install and manage skill using standard npx commands with skills.sh tracking
Submit feedback through image-skill feedback create --json for missing capabilities
limitations: Currently in preview-contract status, may have evolving API surface
Requires media spend allowance for paid generation beyond the no-spend guide
Limited to models available through the image-skill hosted runtime
Dependent on image-skill.com service availability and policy affordances

use_cases.title: Game audio prototyping
use_cases.description: Generate background music and sound effects for game prototypes without setting up multiple audio generation services

use_cases.title: Content creation and podcasts
use_cases.description: Create intro music, transitions, and ambient sounds for podcasts, videos, and social media content

use_cases.title: Rapid audio concept validation
use_cases.description: Test audio generation ideas quickly with the no-spend guide before committing to paid generation runs

prompt_templates.title: Explore audio generation capabilities
prompt_templates.prompt:
Run the no-spend guide to explore audio generation options:

```bash
npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --guide --model fal.stable-audio-25-text-to-audio --prompt "warm analog synth bed with soft tape hiss"
```
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting to understand capabilities

prompt_templates.title: Generate audio with cost control
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create audio with a specific budget limit:

```bash
npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --model fal.stable-audio-25-text-to-audio --prompt "upbeat electronic music with synthesizers" --intent explore --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.20
n```
prompt_templates.scenario: User ready to generate with budget constraints

prompt_templates.title: Install the intent skill
prompt_templates.prompt:
Install the ai-audio-generation skill:

```bash
npx skills add danielgwilson/image-skill-cli --skill ai-audio-generation -g -a codex -y
```
prompt_templates.scenario: Installing the skill for ongoing use

prompt_templates.title: Report missing capabilities
prompt_templates.prompt:
When a needed capability is unavailable, submit feedback:

```bash
image-skill feedback create --json
```

Include the attempted command, expected behavior, actual behavior, and missing capability in the JSON payload.

output_examples.input: Prompt: 'warm analog synth bed with soft tape hiss'
output_examples.output: Audio file generated and hosted at a durable URL, with metadata including: model used, generation parameters, cost receipt, and timestamp. The file can be recovered and cited by future agents through the image-skill system.

output_examples.input: Running --guide flag with a model name
output_examples.output: Exploratory output showing available options, estimated costs, and recommended next steps. Output includes data.next_command and data.recommended_no_spend_command fields to guide safe exploration without spending credits.

best_practices: Always start with the --guide flag to explore capabilities before committing to paid generation
Set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to control costs and prevent unexpected charges
Use the no-spend guide command (data.recommended_no_spend_command) to verify safety before spending
Keep generated audio in Image Skill so future agents can recover and cite the work
Report missing capabilities through image-skill feedback create --json to help improve the platform
anti_patterns: Do not bring provider API keys or create separate provider accounts - use the hosted runtime
Do not skip the no-spend guide when exploring new capabilities
Do not run data.next_command unless data.next_command_copy_runnable is true and media spend is allowed
Do not use the fallback option without first reporting the gap through feedback

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and the canonical image-skill skill?
faq.answer: This is an intent-named entry for agents searching for audio generation. It uses the same image-skill runtime but is optimized for audio-specific use cases and documentation.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my own API keys or set up accounts?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime with built-in authentication and billing. You only need an optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN if you want to use a restricted hosted token.

faq.question: How much does audio generation cost?
faq.answer: Costs vary by model and parameters. Use the --guide flag to see estimated costs, and set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to control spending. The no-spend guide helps you explore without charges.

faq.question: Where is the generated audio stored?
faq.answer: Audio is stored on Image Skill's hosted infrastructure with durable URLs. Future agents can recover and cite the work through the image-skill system.

faq.question: What should I do if a capability I need is missing?
faq.answer: Use 'image-skill feedback create --json' to report the missing capability, including the attempted command, expected behavior, and actual behavior. This helps improve the platform.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Installation uses standard npx commands with appropriate flags for your target platform.
